---
blueprint:
  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Limiter"
  description: >
    Decrements an `input_number` helper each time a Lock Code Manager code
    slot PIN is used on the lock. When the counter reaches 0, the Lock Code
    Manager slot is automatically disabled. Optionally resets the counter
    when the slot is re-enabled in Lock Code Manager. Optionally restricts
    the limit to a specific lock or set of locks.


    **Special values**

    - Set the counter to **-1** for unlimited uses (counter stays at -1,
      slot is never disabled).

    - Set the counter to **0** to immediately disable the slot on next use.


    **Requirements**

    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot events
    (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events). If none of your locks
    support this, the counter will never decrement.


    **Setup**

    1. Create an `input_number` helper (Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers)
       with min=-1, max=your desired limit, and step=1.

    2. Import this blueprint and configure it with the PIN used event entity,
       enabled switch entity, and the helper you just created.
  domain: automation
  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml
  input:
    config_entry:
      name: Lock Code Manager config entry
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager config entry that manages your lock(s).
        Used to identify the setup in notifications.
      selector:
        config_entry:
          integration: lock_code_manager
    slot_number:
      name: Code slot number
      description: The Lock Code Manager code slot number.
      selector:
        number:
          min: 1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    pin_used_entity:
      name: PIN used event entity
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when the PIN is used
        (e.g. `event.front_door_code_slot_1_pin_used`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: event
          integration: lock_code_manager
    locks:
      name: Locks (optional)
      description: >
        Restrict the limit to a specific lock or set of locks. When empty,
        PIN uses on any lock count toward the limit. When one or more locks
        are selected, only uses on those locks decrement the counter — uses
        on other locks are ignored.
      default: []
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: lock
          multiple: true
    enabled_switch:
      name: Slot enabled switch
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager switch entity that enables/disables the code
        slot (e.g. `switch.front_door_code_slot_1_enabled`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: switch
          integration: lock_code_manager
    uses_counter:
      name: Uses counter
      description: >
        An `input_number` helper that tracks remaining uses. Set to -1 for
        unlimited uses. Create one via Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers
        (set min to -1).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: input_number
    initial_uses:
      name: Initial uses on re-enable
      description: >
        When greater than 0, the counter is automatically reset to this value
        each time the Lock Code Manager slot is re-enabled. Set to 0 to
        disable auto-reset. Set to -1 for unlimited uses on re-enable.
      default: 0
      selector:
        number:
          min: -1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    notify_target:
      name: Notification service (optional)
      description: >
        A notify entity to send a message to when uses are exhausted and the
        slot is disabled. Leave empty to skip notifications.
      default: ""
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: notify

mode: queued
max: 10

triggers:
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
    id: pin_used
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input enabled_switch
    id: slot_enabled
    to: "on"

variables:
  initial_uses: !input initial_uses
  uses_counter: !input uses_counter
  notify_target: !input notify_target
  locks: !input locks
  _config_entry: !input config_entry
  _config_entry_title: >
    {{ config_entry_attr(_config_entry, 'title') }}
  _slot_number: !input slot_number

actions:
  - choose:
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: pin_used
          - condition: template
            value_template: >-
              {{ locks | count == 0
                 or trigger.to_state.attributes.event_type in locks }}
        sequence:
          - variables:
              current: >
                {{ states(uses_counter) | int(0) }}
          # -1 means unlimited — skip decrement and disable
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ current == -1 }}"
            then:
              - stop: "Unlimited uses — no action needed"
          - variables:
              new_count: >
                {{ [current - 1, 0] | max }}
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: "{{ new_count }}"
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ new_count == 0 }}"
            then:
              - action: switch.turn_off
                target:
                  entity_id: !input enabled_switch
              - if:
                  - condition: template
                    value_template: "{{ notify_target != '' }}"
                then:
                  - action: notify.send_message
                    target:
                      entity_id: "{{ notify_target }}"
                    data:
                      title: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} — Slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        Usage Limit Reached
                      message: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} code slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        has been disabled after reaching the usage limit.
                        Last used on lock
                        {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')
                           | default('unknown') }}
                        at {{ now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}.
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: slot_enabled
          - condition: template
            value_template: "{{ initial_uses | int(0) != 0 }}"
        sequence:
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: !input initial_uses

aiTitle: Add optional lock parameter to slot notifier blueprint

input.command:
git add blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_notifier.yaml blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Use state_attr for lock entity lookup in slot usage blueprints

Replaces `trigger.to_state.attributes.event_type` with
`state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')` in the lock-filter
templates of the Slot Usage Notifier and Slot Usage Limiter
blueprints. The new form does not rely on `trigger.to_state`
being non-null and matches the existing pattern already used in
the limiter's notification message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin fix/blueprint-lock-lookup-state-attr && gh pr create --title "Use state_attr for lock entity lookup in slot usage blueprints" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Follow-up to #1152 and #1153. In both blueprints, the new lock-filter template referenced `trigger.to_state.attributes.event_type`. This switches both to `state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')`:

- Removes any dependency on `trigger.to_state` being non-null (it can be `None` in edge cases like the entity being removed mid-trigger).
- Matches the pattern already used in the limiter's notification message (`slot_usage_limiter.yaml` line 187 prior to this change), so the file is internally consistent.

No behavioral change for the normal case — the two expressions resolve to the same value when `trigger.to_state` is present.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: #1152, #1153
EOF
)"

Updates `matter-python-client` from 0.6.8 to 0.7.1
Release notes

Sourced from matter-python-client's releases.

Release v0.7.1

Official release, check CHANGELOG.md for details

Release v0.7.0

Official release, check CHANGELOG.md for details

Changelog

Sourced from matter-python-client's changelog.

0.7.1 (2026-05-21)

  • Feature: Added BLE proxy commissioning support (enabled with --ble-proxy CLI option)
  • Enhancement: Dashboard shows Thread protocol version on Thread node details
  • Enhancement: Dashboard always shows Thread/WiFi navigation tabs (removed small-screen gate)
  • Maintenance: Update matter.js to the official 0.17.0
  • Fix: Fix the Docker-Health-Checks when a custom Listen address was used
  • Fix: Dashboard auto-focuses pairing-code field when commission-node dialog opens

0.7.0 (2026-05-18)

  • IMPORTANT: The first start, when coming from any previous version, will take a bit because we migrate the storage to the new "WAL"-based storage format. This is a one-time migration that reduces disk usage and I/O.
  • Breaking: Enables strict validation of Attestation and Certificates at commissioning like the Python server (Test-DCL Mode also checks the official test certificates)
  • Feature: Updates the Matter version to be compatible with Matter 1.5.1
  • Feature: Enables Matter TCP support when devices support it (likely very few devices)
  • Feature: Updates the generated Python client classes to match Matter 1.5.1
  • Feature: Seeds Certificates and Vendor Information to allow basic functionality also without internet access
  • Feature: Enhances the Dashboard UI to allow to clear and change the Wifi/Thread credentials
  • Feature: (iamadamreed) Adds TCL custom cluster
  • Feature: (burmistrzak) Adds "window open mode" attribute for Eve custom cluster
  • Feature: Adds experimental Camera Live View support — WebRTC streaming with snapshot capture, exposed via a Live View button on Camera and Video Doorbell device types.
  • Feature: Adds experimental Dashboard UI for the Camera AV Settings User Level Management cluster (MPTZ controls, presets, DPTZ stream info) plus a compact PTZ strip in the live-view overlay.
  • Feature: Adds experimental Dashboard UI for the Chime cluster (sound selection, play, last-played event readout).
  • Fix: When creating bindings via Dashboard, use the correct permission levels ("Operate" instead of "Administer")
  • Fix: Ensures that also official test certificates are initialized correctly when DCL-Testnet-flag is enabled
  • Fix: Fixed some edge cases in the "Node available" logic to ensure it correctly reflects the node stats
  • Fix: Update matter.js to the latest 0.17.0-nightly
    • Fixes write encoding for some cases of nullable attributes
    • Make scanNetwork failures non-fatal for commissioning
    • Ensures re-subscriptions from the device to reset the device state to "Connected"
    • ... more optimizations and fixes

file.content:
---
# To get started with Dependabot version updates, you'll need to specify which
# package ecosystems to update and where the package manifests are located.
# Please see the documentation for all configuration options:
# https://help.github.com/github/administering-a-repository/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates

version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "github-config"
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "python"
    groups:
      homeassistant:
        patterns:
          - "homeassistant"
          - "matter-python-client"
          - "pytest"
          - "pytest-homeassistant-custom-component"
          - "zeroconf"
          - "zwave-js-server-python"
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "javascript"

aiTitle: Add optional lock parameter to slot notifier blueprint

input.command:
git add .github/dependabot.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Remove matter-python-client and zeroconf from homeassistant group

These packages are transitively pinned by Home Assistant itself, so
grouping them with the `homeassistant` bump causes Dependabot to
propose conflicting version pins (e.g. PR #1146 pinned
zeroconf==0.149.13 while homeassistant==2026.5.3 requires
zeroconf==0.148.0, making the lockfile unresolvable). Splitting them
out lets each be tracked individually without forcing the group
update to fail dependency resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin chore/dependabot-trim-homeassistant-group && gh pr create --title "Remove matter-python-client and zeroconf from Dependabot homeassistant group" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Drops `matter-python-client` and `zeroconf` from the `homeassistant` Dependabot group in `.github/dependabot.yaml`. Both packages are transitively pinned by Home Assistant itself, so grouping them with the `homeassistant` bump regularly produces conflicting version pins.

Concrete example: PR #1146 grouped `homeassistant>=2026.5.3` with `zeroconf==0.149.13`, but `homeassistant==2026.5.3` requires `zeroconf==0.148.0`. The resulting `requirements_dev.txt` was unresolvable, failing pytest on Python 3.14.

After this change, Dependabot will:
- Continue to group `homeassistant`, `pytest`, `pytest-homeassistant-custom-component`, and `zwave-js-server-python` together (they update in lockstep with HA).
- Track `matter-python-client` and `zeroconf` as individual PRs, where their pins can be evaluated against the active `homeassistant` version on a case-by-case basis.

PR #1146 will need to be closed (or rebased after Dependabot regenerates the group) so the unresolvable `zeroconf==0.149.13` pin is dropped.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: #1146
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit, push, open PR for dependabot config change

---
# To get started with Dependabot version updates, you'll need to specify which
# package ecosystems to update and where the package manifests are located.
# Please see the documentation for all configuration options:
# https://help.github.com/github/administering-a-repository/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates

version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "github-config"
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "python"
    groups:
      homeassistant:
        patterns:
          - "homeassistant"
          - "pytest"
          - "pytest-homeassistant-custom-component"
          - "zwave-js-server-python"
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "javascript"

input.command:
git add .github/dependabot.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Also drop pytest and zwave-js-server-python from homeassistant group

Trims the group further so it only contains the two packages whose
versions must move in lockstep: homeassistant itself and
pytest-homeassistant-custom-component (whose releases are tagged
against specific HA versions). pytest and zwave-js-server-python
have independent release cadences and don't benefit from being
batched with HA bumps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push && gh pr edit 1155 --title "Trim Dependabot homeassistant group to HA + pytest-homeassistant-custom-component" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Trims the `homeassistant` Dependabot group in `.github/dependabot.yaml` so it only contains the two packages whose versions are tightly coupled and must move in lockstep:

- `homeassistant`
- `pytest-homeassistant-custom-component`

The following packages are removed from the group and will be tracked as individual Dependabot PRs:

- `matter-python-client` — transitively pinned by HA, was producing conflicting pins.
- `zeroconf` — transitively pinned by HA; conflicting pins broke PR #1146.
- `pytest` — independent release cadence.
- `zwave-js-server-python` — independent release cadence.

### Concrete motivation

PR #1146 grouped `homeassistant>=2026.5.3` with `zeroconf==0.149.13`, but `homeassistant==2026.5.3` requires `zeroconf==0.148.0`. The resulting `requirements_dev.txt` was unresolvable and pytest failed on Python 3.14. Grouping packages that HA itself pins is the root cause of that class of failure.

### Follow-up

PR #1146 will need to be closed (or Dependabot will regenerate the group without the offending packages on its next run).

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: #1146
EOF
)"

file.content:
---
blueprint:
  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Notifier"
  description: >
    Runs one or more actions when a Lock Code Manager code slot PIN
    is used on a lock. Use this to send notifications, trigger scripts,
    or run any other Home Assistant action.


    **How it works**

    When a code slot event fires (PIN used on lock), the automation
    runs the configured actions. You can use the action editor to
    configure any HA action — notifications, scripts, scenes, etc.


    **Available template variables**

    Your actions can reference these variables in templates:

    - `slot_num` — the code slot number that was used

    - `slot_name` — the user-configured name of the code slot

    - `lock_name` — the friendly name of the lock where the PIN
      was used

    - `timestamp` — the date and time the event occurred


    **Requirements**

    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot
    events (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events).


    **Setup**

    1. Import this blueprint and create an automation from it.

    2. Select the code slot event entity to monitor.

    3. Configure the actions to run when the PIN is used.
  domain: automation
  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_notifier.yaml
  input:
    event_entity:
      name: Code slot event entity
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when a PIN
        is used. These are named like "Code slot X PIN used".
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: event
          integration: lock_code_manager
    locks:
      name: Locks (optional)
      description: >
        Restrict the automation to a specific lock or set of locks.
        When empty, the actions run for any lock the event entity
        reports. When one or more locks are selected, the actions
        only run when one of those locks is the lock that used the
        PIN.
      default: []
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: lock
          multiple: true
    notify_actions:
      name: Actions
      description: >
        The actions to run when a PIN is used. You can configure
        any HA action here — notifications, scripts, etc. Use
        template variables like `{{ slot_name }}` and
        `{{ lock_name }}` in your action data.
      selector:
        action:

mode: queued
max: 10

triggers:
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input event_entity
    not_to:
      - unknown
      - unavailable

variables:
  event_entity_id: !input event_entity
  locks: !input locks
  lock_entity_id: >-
    {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type') }}
  slot_name: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.attributes.code_slot_name
       | default('Unknown slot') }}
  slot_num: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.attributes.code_slot
       | default('?') }}
  lock_name: >-
    {{ state_attr(lock_entity_id, 'friendly_name')
       | default('Unknown lock') }}
  timestamp: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.last_updated | as_local
       | as_timestamp | timestamp_custom('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}

conditions:
  - condition: template
    value_template: >-
      {{ locks | count == 0 or lock_entity_id in locks }}

actions: !input notify_actions

---
blueprint:
  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Notifier"
  description: >
    Runs one or more actions when a Lock Code Manager code slot PIN
    is used on a lock. Use this to send notifications, trigger scripts,
    or run any other Home Assistant action.


    **How it works**

    When a code slot event fires (PIN used on lock), the automation
    runs the configured actions. You can use the action editor to
    configure any HA action — notifications, scripts, scenes, etc.


    **Available template variables**

    Your actions can reference these variables in templates:

    - `slot_num` — the code slot number that was used

    - `slot_name` — the user-configured name of the code slot

    - `lock_name` — the friendly name of the lock where the PIN
      was used

    - `timestamp` — the date and time the event occurred


    **Requirements**

    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot
    events (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events).


    **Setup**

    1. Import this blueprint and create an automation from it.

    2. Select one or more code slot event entities to monitor.

    3. Configure the actions to run when the PIN is used.
  domain: automation
  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_notifier.yaml
  input:
    event_entity:
      name: Code slot event entity
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when a PIN
        is used. These are named like "Code slot X PIN used".
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: event
          integration: lock_code_manager
    locks:
      name: Locks (optional)
      description: >
        Restrict the automation to a specific lock or set of locks.
        When empty, the actions run for any lock the event entity
        reports. When one or more locks are selected, the actions
        only run when one of those locks is the lock that used the
        PIN.
      default: []
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: lock
          multiple: true
    notify_actions:
      name: Actions
      description: >
        The actions to run when a PIN is used. You can configure
        any HA action here — notifications, scripts, etc. Use
        template variables like `{{ slot_name }}` and
        `{{ lock_name }}` in your action data.
      selector:
        action:

mode: queued
max: 10

triggers:
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input event_entity
    not_to:
      - unknown
      - unavailable

variables:
  event_entity_id: !input event_entity
  locks: !input locks
  lock_entity_id: >-
    {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type') }}
  slot_name: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.attributes.code_slot_name
       | default('Unknown slot') }}
  slot_num: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.attributes.code_slot
       | default('?') }}
  lock_name: >-
    {{ state_attr(lock_entity_id, 'friendly_name')
       | default('Unknown lock') }}
  timestamp: >-
    {{ trigger.to_state.last_updated | as_local
       | as_timestamp | timestamp_custom('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}

conditions:
  - condition: template
    value_template: >-
      {{ locks | count == 0 or lock_entity_id in locks }}

actions: !input notify_actions

lines: -        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when a PIN
-        is used. These are named like "Code slot X PIN used".
+        One or more Lock Code Manager event entities that fire when a
+        PIN is used. These are named like "Code slot X PIN used". The
+        automation runs whenever any of the selected entities fires.

input.command:
git add blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_notifier.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Allow the Slot Usage Notifier to monitor multiple event entities

Adds `multiple: true` to the `event_entity` selector so the blueprint
can be configured with one or more LCM PIN-used event entities. The
state trigger already accepts a list of entity IDs, so the existing
template variables (which key off the firing entity via
`trigger.entity_id` / `trigger.to_state`) work unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin feat/notifier-multi-event-entity && gh pr create --title "Allow Slot Usage Notifier to monitor multiple event entities" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Adds `multiple: true` to the `event_entity` selector on the Slot Usage Notifier blueprint, so users can configure the same automation to react to PIN-used events from one or more LCM event entities (e.g. several code slots, or the same slot across multiple locks).

The state trigger already accepts a list of entity IDs as `entity_id`, and the template variables (`lock_entity_id`, `slot_name`, `slot_num`, `lock_name`, `timestamp`) all key off the specific firing entity via `trigger.entity_id` / `trigger.to_state`, so they work unchanged for either a single or multi-entity configuration.

Also updates the input name and setup instructions to reflect that one or more entities can be selected.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue:
EOF
)"

message.content: for the slot usage limiter, why can't we derive config entry and slot number from pin_used_entity

content.text:
You can, with two HA template functions:

- `_slot_number` ← `{{ state_attr(pin_used_entity_id, 'code_slot') }}` — LCM stores the slot number as a `code_slot` attribute on the event entity (the notifier already reads this from `trigger.to_state.attributes.code_slot`).
- `_config_entry` ← `{{ config_entry_id(pin_used_entity_id) }}` — every HA entity carries the ID of its source config entry, and LCM-created event entities are registered to the LCM config entry by construction.

The variable block would look like:

```yaml
variables:
  pin_used_entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
  _slot_number: >
    {{ state_attr(pin_used_entity_id, 'code_slot') }}
  _config_entry: >
    {{ config_entry_id(pin_used_entity_id) }}
  _config_entry_title: >
    {{ config_entry_attr(_config_entry, 'title') }}
```

That deletes two inputs from the form. Trade-offs to weigh:

1. **Breaking change for existing automations** — the input keys disappear, so users who imported the old blueprint need to re-save (or re-import). With only #1153 just-merged this is probably very few people.
2. **Tighter coupling to LCM's attribute schema** — if `code_slot` ever gets renamed inside LCM, the blueprint silently breaks at trigger time instead of at setup time. (The notifier already has this coupling, so it's not adding a new fragility class — just expanding it.)
3. **Slightly less obvious form** — the explicit inputs document the blueprint's intent ("this is for slot N of this config entry"). Derived inputs make the form simpler but more "magical."
4. **Validation moves to runtime** — `slot_number`'s `number` selector currently does range validation at setup; with derivation, a wrong/unloaded entity surfaces as a None at trigger time.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The same simplification works for the `enabled_switch` input too: it's `switch.{lock}_code_slot_{N}_enabled`, and LCM exposes it on the same config entry / device. You could derive it via the entity registry by config_entry_id + unique_id pattern — though that's more involved than a single template function, so it's a less clean win than slot_number and config_entry.
- HA's `config_entry_id(entity)` and `config_entry_attr(id, 'title')` are a pretty under-known pair — they let blueprints discover their own context entirely from one user-selected entity, which is exactly the pattern that makes #1159's "pick the event entity, infer the rest" UX feasible.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Want me to do it (one PR replacing the two inputs with derivations)?

1	---
2	blueprint:
3	  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Limiter"
4	  description: >
5	    Decrements an `input_number` helper each time a Lock Code Manager code
6	    slot PIN is used on the lock. When the counter reaches 0, the Lock Code
7	    Manager slot is automatically disabled. Optionally resets the counter
8	    when the slot is re-enabled in Lock Code Manager. Optionally restricts
9	    the limit to a specific lock or set of locks.
10	
11	
12	    **Special values**
13	
14	    - Set the counter to **-1** for unlimited uses (counter stays at -1,
15	      slot is never disabled).
16	
17	    - Set the counter to **0** to immediately disable the slot on next use.
18	
19	
20	    **Requirements**
21	
22	    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot events
23	    (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events). If none of your locks
24	    support this, the counter will never decrement.
25	
26	
27	    **Setup**
28	
29	    1. Create an `input_number` helper (Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers)
30	       with min=-1, max=your desired limit, and step=1.
31	
32	    2. Import this blueprint and configure it with the PIN used event entity,
33	       enabled switch entity, and the helper you just created.
34	  domain: automation
35	  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
36	  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml
37	  input:
38	    config_entry:
39	      name: Lock Code Manager config entry
40	      description: >
41	        The Lock Code Manager config entry that manages your lock(s).
42	        Used to identify the setup in notifications.
43	      selector:
44	        config_entry:
45	          integration: lock_code_manager
46	    slot_number:
47	      name: Code slot number
48	      description: The Lock Code Manager code slot number.
49	      selector:
50	        number:
51	          min: 1
52	          max: 999
53	          mode: box
54	    pin_used_entity:
55	      name: PIN used event entity
56	      description: >
57	        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when the PIN is used
58	        (e.g. `event.front_door_code_slot_1_pin_used`).
59	      selector:
60	        entity:
61	          domain: event
62	          integration: lock_code_manager
63	    locks:
64	      name: Locks (optional)
65	      description: >
66	        Restrict the limit to a specific lock or set of locks. When empty,
67	        PIN uses on any lock count toward the limit. When one or more locks
68	        are selected, only uses on those locks decrement the counter — uses
69	        on other locks are ignored.
70	      default: []
71	      selector:
72	        entity:
73	          domain: lock
74	          multiple: true
75	    enabled_switch:
76	      name: Slot enabled switch
77	      description: >
78	        The Lock Code Manager switch entity that enables/disables the code
79	        slot (e.g. `switch.front_door_code_slot_1_enabled`).
80	      selector:
81	        entity:
82	          domain: switch
83	          integration: lock_code_manager
84	    uses_counter:
85	      name: Uses counter
86	      description: >
87	        An `input_number` helper that tracks remaining uses. Set to -1 for
88	        unlimited uses. Create one via Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers
89	        (set min to -1).
90	      selector:
91	        entity:
92	          domain: input_number
93	    initial_uses:
94	      name: Initial uses on re-enable
95	      description: >
96	        When greater than 0, the counter is automatically reset to this value
97	        each time the Lock Code Manager slot is re-enabled. Set to 0 to
98	        disable auto-reset. Set to -1 for unlimited uses on re-enable.
99	      default: 0
100	      selector:
101	        number:
102	          min: -1
103	          max: 999
104	          mode: box
105	    notify_target:
106	      name: Notification service (optional)
107	      description: >
108	        A notify entity to send a message to when uses are exhausted and the
109	        slot is disabled. Leave empty to skip notifications.
110	      default: ""
111	      selector:
112	        entity:
113	          domain: notify
114	
115	mode: queued
116	max: 10
117	
118	triggers:
119	  - trigger: state
120	    entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
121	    id: pin_used
122	  - trigger: state
123	    entity_id: !input enabled_switch
124	    id: slot_enabled
125	    to: "on"
126	
127	variables:
128	  initial_uses: !input initial_uses
129	  uses_counter: !input uses_counter
130	  notify_target: !input notify_target
131	  locks: !input locks
132	  _config_entry: !input config_entry
133	  _config_entry_title: >
134	    {{ config_entry_attr(_config_entry, 'title') }}
135	  _slot_number: !input slot_number
136	
137	actions:
138	  - choose:
139	      - conditions:
140	          - condition: trigger
141	            id: pin_used
142	          - condition: template
143	            value_template: >-
144	              {{ locks | count == 0
145	                 or state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type') in locks }}
146	        sequence:
147	          - variables:
148	              current: >
149	                {{ states(uses_counter) | int(0) }}
150	          # -1 means unlimited — skip decrement and disable
151	          - if:
152	              - condition: template
153	                value_template: "{{ current == -1 }}"
154	            then:
155	              - stop: "Unlimited uses — no action needed"
156	          - variables:
157	              new_count: >
158	                {{ [current - 1, 0] | max }}
159	          - action: input_number.set_value
160	            target:
161	              entity_id: !input uses_counter
162	            data:
163	              value: "{{ new_count }}"
164	          - if:
165	              - condition: template
166	                value_template: "{{ new_count == 0 }}"
167	            then:
168	              - action: switch.turn_off
169	                target:
170	                  entity_id: !input enabled_switch
171	              - if:
172	                  - condition: template
173	                    value_template: "{{ notify_target != '' }}"
174	                then:
175	                  - action: notify.send_message
176	                    target:
177	                      entity_id: "{{ notify_target }}"
178	                    data:
179	                      title: >
180	                        {{ _config_entry_title }} — Slot {{ _slot_number }}
181	                        Usage Limit Reached
182	                      message: >
183	                        {{ _config_entry_title }} code slot {{ _slot_number }}
184	                        has been disabled after reaching the usage limit.
185	                        Last used on lock
186	                        {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')
187	                           | default('unknown') }}
188	                        at {{ now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}.
189	      - conditions:
190	          - condition: trigger
191	            id: slot_enabled
192	          - condition: template
193	            value_template: "{{ initial_uses | int(0) != 0 }}"
194	        sequence:
195	          - action: input_number.set_value
196	            target:
197	              entity_id: !input uses_counter
198	            data:
199	              value: !input initial_uses

---
blueprint:
  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Limiter"
  description: >
    Decrements an `input_number` helper each time a Lock Code Manager code
    slot PIN is used on the lock. When the counter reaches 0, the Lock Code
    Manager slot is automatically disabled. Optionally resets the counter
    when the slot is re-enabled in Lock Code Manager. Optionally restricts
    the limit to a specific lock or set of locks.


    **Special values**

    - Set the counter to **-1** for unlimited uses (counter stays at -1,
      slot is never disabled).

    - Set the counter to **0** to immediately disable the slot on next use.


    **Requirements**

    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot events
    (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events). If none of your locks
    support this, the counter will never decrement.


    **Setup**

    1. Create an `input_number` helper (Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers)
       with min=-1, max=your desired limit, and step=1.

    2. Import this blueprint and configure it with the PIN used event entity,
       enabled switch entity, and the helper you just created.
  domain: automation
  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml
  input:
    config_entry:
      name: Lock Code Manager config entry
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager config entry that manages your lock(s).
        Used to identify the setup in notifications.
      selector:
        config_entry:
          integration: lock_code_manager
    slot_number:
      name: Code slot number
      description: The Lock Code Manager code slot number.
      selector:
        number:
          min: 1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    pin_used_entity:
      name: PIN used event entity
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when the PIN is used
        (e.g. `event.front_door_code_slot_1_pin_used`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: event
          integration: lock_code_manager
    locks:
      name: Locks (optional)
      description: >
        Restrict the limit to a specific lock or set of locks. When empty,
        PIN uses on any lock count toward the limit. When one or more locks
        are selected, only uses on those locks decrement the counter — uses
        on other locks are ignored.
      default: []
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: lock
          multiple: true
    enabled_switch:
      name: Slot enabled switch
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager switch entity that enables/disables the code
        slot (e.g. `switch.front_door_code_slot_1_enabled`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: switch
          integration: lock_code_manager
    uses_counter:
      name: Uses counter
      description: >
        An `input_number` helper that tracks remaining uses. Set to -1 for
        unlimited uses. Create one via Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers
        (set min to -1).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: input_number
    initial_uses:
      name: Initial uses on re-enable
      description: >
        When greater than 0, the counter is automatically reset to this value
        each time the Lock Code Manager slot is re-enabled. Set to 0 to
        disable auto-reset. Set to -1 for unlimited uses on re-enable.
      default: 0
      selector:
        number:
          min: -1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    notify_target:
      name: Notification service (optional)
      description: >
        A notify entity to send a message to when uses are exhausted and the
        slot is disabled. Leave empty to skip notifications.
      default: ""
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: notify

mode: queued
max: 10

triggers:
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
    id: pin_used
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input enabled_switch
    id: slot_enabled
    to: "on"

variables:
  initial_uses: !input initial_uses
  uses_counter: !input uses_counter
  notify_target: !input notify_target
  locks: !input locks
  _config_entry: !input config_entry
  _config_entry_title: >
    {{ config_entry_attr(_config_entry, 'title') }}
  _slot_number: !input slot_number

actions:
  - choose:
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: pin_used
          - condition: template
            value_template: >-
              {{ locks | count == 0
                 or state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type') in locks }}
        sequence:
          - variables:
              current: >
                {{ states(uses_counter) | int(0) }}
          # -1 means unlimited — skip decrement and disable
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ current == -1 }}"
            then:
              - stop: "Unlimited uses — no action needed"
          - variables:
              new_count: >
                {{ [current - 1, 0] | max }}
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: "{{ new_count }}"
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ new_count == 0 }}"
            then:
              - action: switch.turn_off
                target:
                  entity_id: !input enabled_switch
              - if:
                  - condition: template
                    value_template: "{{ notify_target != '' }}"
                then:
                  - action: notify.send_message
                    target:
                      entity_id: "{{ notify_target }}"
                    data:
                      title: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} — Slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        Usage Limit Reached
                      message: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} code slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        has been disabled after reaching the usage limit.
                        Last used on lock
                        {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')
                           | default('unknown') }}
                        at {{ now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}.
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: slot_enabled
          - condition: template
            value_template: "{{ initial_uses | int(0) != 0 }}"
        sequence:
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: !input initial_uses

      name: Lock Code Manager config entry
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager config entry that manages your lock(s).
        Used to identify the setup in notifications.
      selector:
        config_entry:

    slot_number:
      name: Code slot number
      description: The Lock Code Manager code slot number.
      selector:
        number:
          min: 1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    pin_used_entity:

139	              - condition: template
140	                value_template: "{{ current == -1 }}"
141	            then:
142	              - stop: "Unlimited uses — no action needed"
143	          - variables:

145	                {{ [current - 1, 0] | max }}
146	          - action: input_number.set_value
147	            target:
148	              entity_id: !input uses_counter

153	                value_template: "{{ new_count == 0 }}"
154	            then:

174	                           | default('unknown') }}

file.content:
---
blueprint:
  name: "Lock Code Manager: Slot Usage Limiter"
  description: >
    Decrements an `input_number` helper each time a Lock Code Manager code
    slot PIN is used on the lock. When the counter reaches 0, the Lock Code
    Manager slot is automatically disabled. Optionally resets the counter
    when the slot is re-enabled in Lock Code Manager. Optionally restricts
    the limit to a specific lock or set of locks.


    **Special values**

    - Set the counter to **-1** for unlimited uses (counter stays at -1,
      slot is never disabled).

    - Set the counter to **0** to immediately disable the slot on next use.


    **Requirements**

    This blueprint requires at least one lock that supports code slot events
    (i.e. produces `*_code_slot_*_pin_used` events). If none of your locks
    support this, the counter will never decrement.


    **Setup**

    1. Create an `input_number` helper (Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers)
       with min=-1, max=your desired limit, and step=1.

    2. Import this blueprint and configure it with the PIN used event entity,
       enabled switch entity, and the helper you just created.
  domain: automation
  # yamllint disable-line rule:line-length
  source_url: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/blob/main/blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml
  input:
    pin_used_entity:
      name: PIN used event entity
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager event entity that fires when the PIN is used
        (e.g. `event.front_door_code_slot_1_pin_used`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: event
          integration: lock_code_manager
    locks:
      name: Locks (optional)
      description: >
        Restrict the limit to a specific lock or set of locks. When empty,
        PIN uses on any lock count toward the limit. When one or more locks
        are selected, only uses on those locks decrement the counter — uses
        on other locks are ignored.
      default: []
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: lock
          multiple: true
    enabled_switch:
      name: Slot enabled switch
      description: >
        The Lock Code Manager switch entity that enables/disables the code
        slot (e.g. `switch.front_door_code_slot_1_enabled`).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: switch
          integration: lock_code_manager
    uses_counter:
      name: Uses counter
      description: >
        An `input_number` helper that tracks remaining uses. Set to -1 for
        unlimited uses. Create one via Settings → Devices & Services → Helpers
        (set min to -1).
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: input_number
    initial_uses:
      name: Initial uses on re-enable
      description: >
        When greater than 0, the counter is automatically reset to this value
        each time the Lock Code Manager slot is re-enabled. Set to 0 to
        disable auto-reset. Set to -1 for unlimited uses on re-enable.
      default: 0
      selector:
        number:
          min: -1
          max: 999
          mode: box
    notify_target:
      name: Notification service (optional)
      description: >
        A notify entity to send a message to when uses are exhausted and the
        slot is disabled. Leave empty to skip notifications.
      default: ""
      selector:
        entity:
          domain: notify

mode: queued
max: 10

triggers:
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
    id: pin_used
  - trigger: state
    entity_id: !input enabled_switch
    id: slot_enabled
    to: "on"

variables:
  initial_uses: !input initial_uses
  uses_counter: !input uses_counter
  notify_target: !input notify_target
  locks: !input locks
  pin_used_entity_id: !input pin_used_entity
  _config_entry: >
    {{ config_entry_id(pin_used_entity_id) }}
  _config_entry_title: >
    {{ config_entry_attr(_config_entry, 'title') }}
  _slot_number: >
    {{ state_attr(pin_used_entity_id, 'code_slot') }}

actions:
  - choose:
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: pin_used
          - condition: template
            value_template: >-
              {{ locks | count == 0
                 or state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type') in locks }}
        sequence:
          - variables:
              current: >
                {{ states(uses_counter) | int(0) }}
          # -1 means unlimited — skip decrement and disable
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ current == -1 }}"
            then:
              - stop: "Unlimited uses — no action needed"
          - variables:
              new_count: >
                {{ [current - 1, 0] | max }}
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: "{{ new_count }}"
          - if:
              - condition: template
                value_template: "{{ new_count == 0 }}"
            then:
              - action: switch.turn_off
                target:
                  entity_id: !input enabled_switch
              - if:
                  - condition: template
                    value_template: "{{ notify_target != '' }}"
                then:
                  - action: notify.send_message
                    target:
                      entity_id: "{{ notify_target }}"
                    data:
                      title: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} — Slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        Usage Limit Reached
                      message: >
                        {{ _config_entry_title }} code slot {{ _slot_number }}
                        has been disabled after reaching the usage limit.
                        Last used on lock
                        {{ state_attr(trigger.entity_id, 'event_type')
                           | default('unknown') }}
                        at {{ now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}.
      - conditions:
          - condition: trigger
            id: slot_enabled
          - condition: template
            value_template: "{{ initial_uses | int(0) != 0 }}"
        sequence:
          - action: input_number.set_value
            target:
              entity_id: !input uses_counter
            data:
              value: !input initial_uses

input.command:
git add blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/slot_usage_limiter.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Derive config_entry and slot_number from the PIN used event entity

Removes the explicit `config_entry` and `slot_number` inputs from the
Slot Usage Limiter blueprint and derives both from the
`pin_used_entity` input using `config_entry_id()` and
`state_attr(..., 'code_slot')`. LCM-created event entities are
registered to the LCM config entry by construction, and they carry
the slot number as a `code_slot` attribute, so both values are
available without asking the user.

This reduces the blueprint form from three coupled inputs to one.

BREAKING CHANGE: existing automations built from the prior version
of this blueprint must be re-imported or re-saved — the
`config_entry` and `slot_number` input keys no longer exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin refactor/limiter-derive-from-event-entity && gh pr create --title "Derive config_entry and slot_number from PIN used event entity in Slot Usage Limiter" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
# Breaking change

Existing automations built from the prior version of the **Slot Usage Limiter** blueprint must be re-imported or re-saved after this change. The `config_entry` and `slot_number` input keys are removed; their values are now derived from the `pin_used_entity` input.

## Proposed change

Removes the explicit `config_entry` and `slot_number` inputs from the Slot Usage Limiter blueprint and derives both from the user's `pin_used_entity` selection:

- `_config_entry` ← `config_entry_id(pin_used_entity)` — LCM-created event entities are registered to the LCM config entry by construction, so this returns the correct config entry ID without asking.
- `_slot_number` ← `state_attr(pin_used_entity, 'code_slot')` — LCM stores the slot number as a `code_slot` attribute on the event entity (the same attribute the Slot Usage Notifier already reads).

The form now asks for one PIN-used entity instead of three coupled inputs (config entry + slot number + entity), removing a class of "wrong slot number for the entity you picked" misconfigurations.

### Trade-offs accepted

- **Tighter coupling to LCM's event-entity attribute schema** — if LCM ever renames the `code_slot` attribute, this blueprint breaks at trigger time. The Slot Usage Notifier already has the same coupling, so this is not a new fragility class.
- **Validation moves from setup to runtime** — the explicit `number` selector previously enforced `1 ≤ slot ≤ 999` at setup; an invalid/unloaded entity now surfaces only when the trigger fires.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [x] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue:
EOF
)"

1	"""Test built-in blueprints."""
2	
3	import asyncio
4	from collections.abc import Iterator
5	import contextlib
6	from datetime import timedelta
7	from os import PathLike
8	import pathlib
9	from typing import Any
10	from unittest.mock import patch
11	
12	import pytest
13	
14	from homeassistant.components import automation
15	from homeassistant.components.blueprint import models
16	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntryState
17	from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant, callback
18	from homeassistant.helpers import device_registry as dr
19	from homeassistant.setup import async_setup_component
20	from homeassistant.util import dt as dt_util, yaml as yaml_util
21	
22	from tests.common import MockConfigEntry, async_fire_time_changed, async_mock_service
23	
24	BUILTIN_BLUEPRINT_FOLDER = pathlib.Path(automation.__file__).parent / "blueprints"
25	
26	
27	@contextlib.contextmanager
28	def patch_blueprint(
29	    blueprint_path: str, data_path: str | PathLike[str]
30	) -> Iterator[None]:
31	    """Patch blueprint loading from a different source."""
32	    orig_load = models.DomainBlueprints._load_blueprint
33	
34	    @callback
35	    def mock_load_blueprint(self, path):
36	        if path != blueprint_path:
37	            pytest.fail(f"Unexpected blueprint {path}")
38	            return orig_load(self, path)
39	
40	        return models.Blueprint(
41	            yaml_util.load_yaml(data_path),
42	            expected_domain=self.domain,
43	            path=path,
44	            schema=automation.config.AUTOMATION_BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA,
45	        )
46	
47	    with patch(
48	        "homeassistant.components.blueprint.models.DomainBlueprints._load_blueprint",
49	        mock_load_blueprint,
50	    ):
51	        yield
52	
53	
54	async def test_notify_leaving_zone(
55	    hass: HomeAssistant, device_registry: dr.DeviceRegistry
56	) -> None:
57	    """Test notifying leaving a zone blueprint."""
58	    config_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="fake_integration", data={})
59	    config_entry.mock_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED)
60	    config_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
61	
62	    device = device_registry.async_get_or_create(
63	        config_entry_id=config_entry.entry_id,
64	        connections={(dr.CONNECTION_NETWORK_MAC, "00:00:00:00:00:01")},
65	    )
66	
67	    def set_person_state(state: str, extra: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
68	        hass.states.async_set(
69	            "person.test_person", state, {"friendly_name": "Paulus", **extra}
70	        )
71	
72	    set_person_state("School", {})
73	
74	    assert await async_setup_component(
75	        hass, "zone", {"zone": {"name": "School", "latitude": 1, "longitude": 2}}
76	    )
77	
78	    with patch_blueprint(
79	        "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
80	        BUILTIN_BLUEPRINT_FOLDER / "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
81	    ):
82	        assert await async_setup_component(
83	            hass,
84	            "automation",
85	            {
86	                "automation": {
87	                    "use_blueprint": {
88	                        "path": "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
89	                        "input": {
90	                            "person_entity": "person.test_person",
91	                            "zone_entity": "zone.school",
92	                            "notify_device": device.id,
93	                        },
94	                    }
95	                }
96	            },
97	        )
98	
99	    with patch(
100	        "homeassistant.components.mobile_app.device_action.async_call_action_from_config"
101	    ) as mock_call_action:
102	        # Leaving zone to no zone
103	        set_person_state("not_home", {})
104	        await hass.async_block_till_done()
105	
106	        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 1
107	        _hass, config, variables, _context = mock_call_action.mock_calls[0][1]
108	        message_tpl = config.pop("message")
109	        assert config == {
110	            "alias": "Notify that a person has left the zone",
111	            "domain": "mobile_app",
112	            "type": "notify",
113	            "device_id": device.id,
114	        }
115	        message_tpl.hass = hass
116	        assert message_tpl.async_render(variables) == "Paulus has left School"
117

"""Test built-in blueprints."""

import asyncio
from collections.abc import Iterator
import contextlib
from datetime import timedelta
from os import PathLike
import pathlib
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest

from homeassistant.components import automation
from homeassistant.components.blueprint import models
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntryState
from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant, callback
from homeassistant.helpers import device_registry as dr
from homeassistant.setup import async_setup_component
from homeassistant.util import dt as dt_util, yaml as yaml_util

from tests.common import MockConfigEntry, async_fire_time_changed, async_mock_service

BUILTIN_BLUEPRINT_FOLDER = pathlib.Path(automation.__file__).parent / "blueprints"


@contextlib.contextmanager
def patch_blueprint(
    blueprint_path: str, data_path: str | PathLike[str]
) -> Iterator[None]:
    """Patch blueprint loading from a different source."""
    orig_load = models.DomainBlueprints._load_blueprint

    @callback
    def mock_load_blueprint(self, path):
        if path != blueprint_path:
            pytest.fail(f"Unexpected blueprint {path}")
            return orig_load(self, path)

        return models.Blueprint(
            yaml_util.load_yaml(data_path),
            expected_domain=self.domain,
            path=path,
            schema=automation.config.AUTOMATION_BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA,
        )

    with patch(
        "homeassistant.components.blueprint.models.DomainBlueprints._load_blueprint",
        mock_load_blueprint,
    ):
        yield


async def test_notify_leaving_zone(
    hass: HomeAssistant, device_registry: dr.DeviceRegistry
) -> None:
    """Test notifying leaving a zone blueprint."""
    config_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="fake_integration", data={})
    config_entry.mock_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED)
    config_entry.add_to_hass(hass)

    device = device_registry.async_get_or_create(
        config_entry_id=config_entry.entry_id,
        connections={(dr.CONNECTION_NETWORK_MAC, "00:00:00:00:00:01")},
    )

    def set_person_state(state: str, extra: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        hass.states.async_set(
            "person.test_person", state, {"friendly_name": "Paulus", **extra}
        )

    set_person_state("School", {})

    assert await async_setup_component(
        hass, "zone", {"zone": {"name": "School", "latitude": 1, "longitude": 2}}
    )

    with patch_blueprint(
        "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
        BUILTIN_BLUEPRINT_FOLDER / "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
    ):
        assert await async_setup_component(
            hass,
            "automation",
            {
                "automation": {
                    "use_blueprint": {
                        "path": "notify_leaving_zone.yaml",
                        "input": {
                            "person_entity": "person.test_person",
                            "zone_entity": "zone.school",
                            "notify_device": device.id,
                        },
                    }
                }
            },
        )

    with patch(
        "homeassistant.components.mobile_app.device_action.async_call_action_from_config"
    ) as mock_call_action:
        # Leaving zone to no zone
        set_person_state("not_home", {})
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 1
        _hass, config, variables, _context = mock_call_action.mock_calls[0][1]
        message_tpl = config.pop("message")
        assert config == {
            "alias": "Notify that a person has left the zone",
            "domain": "mobile_app",
            "type": "notify",
            "device_id": device.id,
        }
        message_tpl.hass = hass
        assert message_tpl.async_render(variables) == "Paulus has left School"

        # Should not increase when we go to another zone
        set_person_state("bla", {})
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 1

        # Should not increase when we go into the zone
        set_person_state("School", {})
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 1

        # Should not increase when we move in the zone
        set_person_state("School", {"extra_key": "triggers change with same state"})
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 1

        # Should increase when leaving zone for another zone
        set_person_state("Just Outside School", {})
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 2

        # Verify trigger works
        await hass.services.async_call(
            "automation",
            "trigger",
            {"entity_id": "automation.automation_0"},
            blocking=True,
        )
        assert len(mock_call_action.mock_calls) == 3


async def test_motion_light(hass: HomeAssistant) -> None:
    """Test motion light blueprint."""
    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "off")

    with patch_blueprint(
        "motion_light.yaml",
        BUILTIN_BLUEPRINT_FOLDER / "motion_light.yaml",
    ):
        assert await async_setup_component(
            hass,
            "automation",
            {
                "automation": {
                    "use_blueprint": {
                        "path": "motion_light.yaml",
                        "input": {
                            "light_target": {"entity_id": "light.kitchen"},
                            "motion_entity": "binary_sensor.kitchen",
                        },
                    }
                }
            },
        )

    turn_on_calls = async_mock_service(hass, "light", "turn_on")
    turn_off_calls = async_mock_service(hass, "light", "turn_off")

    # Turn on motion
    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "on")
    # Can't block till done because delay is active
    # So wait 10 event loop iterations to process script
    for _ in range(10):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    assert len(turn_on_calls) == 1

    # Test light doesn't turn off if motion stays
    async_fire_time_changed(hass, dt_util.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=200))

    for _ in range(10):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    assert len(turn_off_calls) == 0

    # Test light turns off off 120s after last motion
    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "off")

    for _ in range(10):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    async_fire_time_changed(hass, dt_util.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=120))
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    assert len(turn_off_calls) == 1

    # Test restarting the script
    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "on")

    for _ in range(10):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    assert len(turn_on_calls) == 2
    assert len(turn_off_calls) == 1

    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "off")

    for _ in range(10):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    hass.states.async_set("binary_sensor.kitchen", "on")

    for _ in range(15):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)

    assert len(turn_on_calls) == 3
    assert len(turn_off_calls) == 1

    # Verify trigger works
    await hass.services.async_call(
        "automation",
        "trigger",
        {"entity_id": "automation.automation_0"},
    )
    for _ in range(25):
        await asyncio.sleep(0)
    assert len(turn_on_calls) == 4

file.content:
"""Test default blueprints."""

import importlib
import logging
import pathlib

import pytest

from homeassistant.components.blueprint import BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA, models
from homeassistant.components.blueprint.const import BLUEPRINT_FOLDER
from homeassistant.util import yaml as yaml_util

DOMAINS = ["automation"]
LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("domain", DOMAINS)
def test_default_blueprints(domain: str) -> None:
    """Validate a folder of blueprints."""
    integration = importlib.import_module(f"homeassistant.components.{domain}")
    blueprint_folder = pathlib.Path(integration.__file__).parent / BLUEPRINT_FOLDER
    items = list(blueprint_folder.glob("*"))
    assert len(items) > 0, "Folder cannot be empty"

    for fil in items:
        LOGGER.info("Processing %s", fil)
        assert fil.name.endswith(".yaml")
        data = yaml_util.load_yaml(fil)
        models.Blueprint(data, expected_domain=domain, schema=BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA)

content.text:
Two patterns in HA core, both portable to LCM:

**1. Schema validation** (`tests/components/blueprint/test_default_blueprints.py`)
30-line test that walks the blueprint folder and runs each YAML through `models.Blueprint(...)`. Cheap, fast, catches malformed selectors / bad input keys at CI time. Wouldn't have caught Copilot's `to_state` nit (that's runtime), but would catch e.g. unknown selector keys or schema typos.

**2. Behavioral tests** (`tests/components/automation/test_blueprint.py`)
Per-blueprint. Pattern is:
- `patch_blueprint("name.yaml", path_to_yaml)` — intercepts `DomainBlueprints._load_blueprint` so HA loads from the custom_components path.
- `async_setup_component(hass, "automation", {"automation": {"use_blueprint": {"path": ..., "input": {...}}}})` — instantiates the blueprint with concrete inputs.
- Trigger by setting state: `hass.states.async_set(entity_id, new_state, attrs)`.
- Assert on the side effect via `async_mock_service(hass, "notify", "send_message")` or similar.

**Fit for LCM specifically — what we have and what we'd need:**
- `tests/conftest.py` already gives us `MockLCMLock`, `MockLockEntity`, `MockConfigEntry`, the `pytest_homeassistant_custom_component` plugin, and the custom-integrations auto-enable fixture. So setup scaffolding is mostly there.
- For the notifier/limiter blueprints, the fake event entity needs the right attributes (`code_slot`, `code_slot_name`, `event_type`) on its state — `async_set` handles that.
- For PR #1160's derivation to be properly exercised, the event entity must be registered to an LCM config entry in the entity registry, not just exist as a state — so the test would spin up a real LCM setup via the existing fixtures, then assert that the blueprint discovers `_config_entry` / `_slot_number` correctly without those being passed as inputs.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- HA's `patch_blueprint` is the load-time hook — it doesn't require the blueprint to live in HA's bundled-blueprints folder. We point it at `blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/*.yaml` directly from the repo.
- The schema test is a near-free win — it'd catch a regression like "I renamed a selector key" within seconds, and would have caught the `to_state` form had it been syntactically wrong (it wasn't). The behavioral tests are where real validation happens: PR #1160's derivation, the lock-filter from #1153, the multi-entity trigger from #1159 — all are observable via mock service calls.
- One LCM-specific pitfall: PR #1160 reads `state_attr(pin_used_entity, 'code_slot')` at variable-eval time, but `state_attr` returns the *current* state — if the test fires a single state change, the variable resolves correctly. If we ever wrote the blueprint to read a historical attribute, the test wouldn't catch it.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Recommendation: do this as two PRs.

- **PR A** — schema test (`tests/test_blueprints.py`, ~30 lines, parametrized over `blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/*.yaml`). Low risk, immediate signal. Fits the project's "tests run quickly" CLAUDE.md guidance trivially.
- **PR B** — behavioral tests, one test class per blueprint, exercising the input matrix that actually matters (lock filter on/off, multi-entity trigger, derived `_slot_number` matching, counter exhaustion, re-enable reset). Worth its own PR because it's where bugs live.

Want me to start with PR A?

(3) mcp__mcphub__mempalace-mempalace_kg_add for atomic facts. (4) mcp__mcphub__mempalace-mempalace_create_tunnel for entities that bridge wings. After saving, the next stop will pass through (stop_hook_active=true).

"""Test utility functions."""

import re

import pytest

from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import async_get as async_get_issue_registry

from custom_components.lock_code_manager.const import DOMAIN
from custom_components.lock_code_manager.util import (
    async_disable_slot,
    mask_pin,
)

INSTANCE_ID = "test-instance-uuid"
LOCK = "lock.front_door"


@pytest.mark.parametrize("pin", ["1234", "5678", "0"])
def test_mask_pin_returns_hash(pin: str):
    """Test mask_pin returns a pin# prefixed 8-char lowercase hex hash."""
    result = mask_pin(pin, LOCK, INSTANCE_ID)
    assert re.fullmatch(r"pin#[0-9a-f]{8}", result), f"Unexpected format: {result}"


def test_mask_pin_deterministic():
    """Test same PIN + lock always produces the same hash."""
    assert mask_pin("1234", LOCK, INSTANCE_ID) == mask_pin("1234", LOCK, INSTANCE_ID)

"""Test utility functions."""

import re

import pytest

from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import async_get as async_get_issue_registry

from custom_components.lock_code_manager.const import DOMAIN
from custom_components.lock_code_manager.util import (
    async_disable_slot,
    mask_pin,
)

INSTANCE_ID = "test-instance-uuid"
LOCK = "lock.front_door"


@pytest.mark.parametrize("pin", ["1234", "5678", "0"])
def test_mask_pin_returns_hash(pin: str):
    """Test mask_pin returns a pin# prefixed 8-char lowercase hex hash."""
    result = mask_pin(pin, LOCK, INSTANCE_ID)
    assert re.fullmatch(r"pin#[0-9a-f]{8}", result), f"Unexpected format: {result}"


def test_mask_pin_deterministic():
    """Test same PIN + lock always produces the same hash."""
    assert mask_pin("1234", LOCK, INSTANCE_ID) == mask_pin("1234", LOCK, INSTANCE_ID)

"""Validate that every shipped blueprint parses against the Home Assistant schema."""

from __future__ import annotations

import pathlib

import pytest

from homeassistant.components.blueprint import BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA, models
from homeassistant.util import yaml as yaml_util

BLUEPRINT_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "blueprints"


def _discover_blueprints() -> list[pytest.ParameterSet]:
    """Discover every (domain, path) blueprint pair shipped in the repo."""
    return [
        pytest.param(path.parent.parent.name, path, id=f"{path.parent.parent.name}/{path.name}")
        for path in sorted(BLUEPRINT_ROOT.glob("*/lock_code_manager/*.yaml"))
    ]


@pytest.mark.parametrize(("domain", "blueprint_path"), _discover_blueprints())
def test_blueprint_schema(domain: str, blueprint_path: pathlib.Path) -> None:
    """Load each blueprint and assert it conforms to the blueprint schema."""
    data = yaml_util.load_yaml(blueprint_path)
    models.Blueprint(
        data,
        expected_domain=domain,
        path=str(blueprint_path),
        schema=BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA,
    )

input.command:
git add tests/test_blueprints.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add schema validation test for shipped blueprints

Walks blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/ and
blueprints/template/lock_code_manager/, loads each YAML file, and
validates it against the Home Assistant BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA. Each
blueprint is a separately-parametrized test case so failures point
at the specific file.

Catches regressions like unknown selector keys, renamed inputs, or
top-level structural errors at CI time. Runs in <1s with no hass
fixture required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin test/blueprint-schema-validation && gh pr create --title "Add schema validation test for shipped blueprints" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Adds `tests/test_blueprints.py`, which discovers every YAML file under `blueprints/automation/lock_code_manager/` and `blueprints/template/lock_code_manager/`, loads each one, and asserts it parses against Home Assistant's `BLUEPRINT_SCHEMA`. Each blueprint is a separately-parametrized test case so a failure points directly at the offending file.

This is the same pattern Home Assistant core uses in `tests/components/blueprint/test_default_blueprints.py`. It catches a specific regression class — unknown selector keys, renamed inputs, schema-level typos — at CI time, before users see them.

Runtime: ~0.2s for all 8 blueprints. No `hass` fixture required, so it doesn't add to the integration-test wall time.

Behavioral tests (firing triggers, asserting on side effects) are intentionally out of scope here and will follow in a separate PR.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue:
EOF
)"

10	from homeassistant.util import yaml as yaml_util
11	
12	BLUEPRINT_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "blueprints"
13	
14	
15	def _discover_blueprints() -> list[pytest.ParameterSet]:
16	    """Discover every (domain, path) blueprint pair shipped in the repo."""
17	    return [
18	        pytest.param(
19	            path.parent.parent.name, path, id=f"{path.parent.parent.name}/{path.name}"
20	        )
21	        for path in sorted(BLUEPRINT_ROOT.glob("*/lock_code_manager/*.yaml"))
22	    ]
23	
24	
25	@pytest.mark.parametrize(("domain", "blueprint_path"), _discover_blueprints())
26	def test_blueprint_schema(domain: str, blueprint_path: pathlib.Path) -> None:
27	    """Load each blueprint and assert it conforms to the blueprint schema."""
28	    data = yaml_util.load_yaml(blueprint_path)
